weather.summary: Saturday: 10°C, clear sky. Sunday: 11°C, scattered clouds

metadata.greeting: This weekend is shaping up to be fantastic, especially with the clear skies predicted for Saturday! I've uncovered a mix of free and engaging activities, from exploring the Horniman Museum's collections to enjoying interactive storytelling sessions. Get ready for a fun-filled weekend, and remember to book ahead where necessary!

activities.title: Library Club at the Hackney Showroom
activities.description: Reading corner and diverse book-pairing service for children of all ages.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a calm and engaging environment for young children with a diverse selection of books. The drop-in nature means no need to book.

transport.fromHackney: Buses and Overground to Hackney Downs or Hackney Central, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Check term times before you go!

activities.title: Inside Aardman: Wallace & Gromit and Friends
activities.description: Family-oriented exhibition showcasing the iconic animated characters from Aardman, from idea development and storyboarding to model making and filming.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive and visually stimulating, perfect for engaging young children with familiar characters.

transport.fromHackney: Central line to Bethnal Green, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Allow ample time as there's lots to see.

activities.title: Sound Explorers at Young V&A
activities.description: Listen, play and make some noise in this informal session.
activities.whyItWorks: Specifically designed for very young children, encouraging sensory exploration and musical play.

transport.fromHackney: Central line to Bethnal Green, then a short walk.
transport.tip: First-come, first-served basis, so arrive early.

activities.title: Museum of London Docklands
activities.description: Discover the history of London's waterfront through interactive exhibits. There is also a Soft Play area!
activities.whyItWorks: The interactive exhibits and soft play area make it a great option for toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Jubilee Line to Canary Wharf.
transport.tip: Check for special events and activities.

activities.title: Queen Elizabeth Olympic Park
activities.description: Let your little one splash about in the fountains and playgrounds.
activities.whyItWorks: The fountains and playgrounds offer lots of space for active play.

transport.fromHackney: Various options including bus, Overground and tube to Stratford.
transport.tip: Pack a change of clothes for fountain fun!

activities.title: Spitalfields City Farm
activities.description: A hidden gem near Brick Lane with a community feel. Check out their line up of activities.
activities.whyItWorks: Kids love animals, and this city farm offers a chance to see goats, pigs, chickens, and more.

transport.fromHackney: Buses and Overground to Shoreditch High Street, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Check for special events and activities.

activities.title: Discover Children's Story Centre
activities.description: Immersive play space with Story Worlds and Story Garden playground. Baby Storytelling plus crafts and singalong time for babies and toddlers.
activities.whyItWorks: Specifically designed for young children, with engaging storytelling and interactive play.

transport.fromHackney: Central and Jubilee lines to Stratford.
transport.tip: Check the schedule for baby and toddler specific sessions.

activities.title: East London Parks Exploration
activities.description: Explore local parks like Victoria Park and enjoy the ponds, play areas with giant slides and a paddling pool.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a chance to run around and enjoy the outdoors, with specific areas designed for young children.

transport.fromHackney: Various bus routes and walking distances.
transport.tip: Pack a picnic and enjoy the day!

activities.title: Natural History Museum
activities.description: Explore the natural world with dinosaur skeletons, interactive exhibits, and family-friendly activities. Celebrate spring with family-friendly Easter activities exploring nature and biodiversity!
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a mix of education and entertainment, with lots to capture the imagination of young children.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria Line to Green Park, then Piccadilly Line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Book ahead for timed entry, even though it's free.

activities.title: Science Museum
activities.description: Discover how the world works with galleries dedicated to space exploration, technology, and more. The Garden is an interactive space where children can discover construction, water, light and sound through play.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive exhibits make learning fun, especially for young children.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria Line to Green Park, then Piccadilly Line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Check for special events and demonstrations.

activities.title: Horniman Museum and Gardens
activities.description: Explore the main gallery with taxidermy animals, enjoy free workshops for children, and visit the beautiful Horniman Gardens with views over London, farm animals, and a music laboratory.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a mix of indoor and outdoor activities, with animals, gardens and hands-on exhibits.

transport.fromHackney: Overground to Forest Hill.
transport.tip: Check for special events and demonstrations.

activities.title: Hyde Park
activities.description: Take a stroll along the Serpentine, and toddlers will love to look at the ducks! Join in with a Discovery Days: Spring Surprises workshop at Hyde Park Learning Centre! Enjoy free, family-friendly activities like pond dipping, nature trails, making seedballs, and more.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a classic London experience, with lots of space to run around and explore.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Central Line to various stops.
transport.tip: Check for special events and demonstrations.

activities.title: British Museum
activities.description: Explore collections from around the world since Antiquity, including Egyptian galleries with mummies and statues.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a chance to learn about different cultures and see fascinating artifacts.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Central Line to Tottenham Court Road or Northern Line to Goodge Street.
transport.tip: Focus on a specific area to avoid overwhelm.

activities.title: Diana Memorial Playground
activities.description: A Peter Pan-inspired play park with a wooden pirate ship.
activities.whyItWorks: The pirate ship and sensory trails offer lots of opportunities for imaginative play.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Central Line to Notting Hill Gate or Bayswater.
transport.tip: Can get crowded, so arrive early.

activities.title: Southbank Centre & Riverside Walks
activities.description: Take a scenic stroll along the Southbank, enjoying street performers, food stalls, and art installations.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a vibrant and engaging atmosphere with lots to see and do.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria Line to Embankment, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Check for free events and activities.

activities.title: Sky Garden
activities.description: A stunning indoor garden with panoramic views of the city.
activities.whyItWorks: The greenery and plants make it a sensory experience, and the views are impressive.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Shoreditch High Street, then Circle or District Line to Monument.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance as it's very popular.

activities.title: Disney on Ice: Into the Magic
activities.description: Be inspired by Disney's Magical characters.
activities.whyItWorks: A visually spectacular show that will appeal to Disney fans.

transport.fromHackney: Jubilee Line to Wembley Park.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance for best prices.

activities.title: Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt at Hampton Court Palace
activities.description: Roam around the gardens and find hidden Lindt Gold Bunny statues to win a chocolate treat.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines outdoor exploration with a fun treasure hunt and chocolate reward.

transport.fromHackney: Train from Liverpool Street to Hampton Court.
transport.tip: Book palace tickets in advance.

activities.title: Playing Shakespeare: Romeo and Juliet
activities.description: Critically acclaimed 90-min version created especially for young people. (Plus Romeo & Juliet Family Workshop (9-11) 28 Mar-11 Apr, £10.)
activities.whyItWorks: A shorter, more accessible version of Shakespeare for young audiences.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria Line to London Bridge, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Octonauts Above & Beyond at Kew Gardens
activities.description: Enjoy a fun-filled family Easter adventure where 3-6s explore a series of installations to find the bees, build them a new hive and help get them there safely.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive installations and a focus on nature will engage young children.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Richmond, then District Line to Kew Gardens.
transport.tip: Book gardens tickets in advance.

activities.title: Future London at London Transport Museum
activities.description: Take part in fun family activities, creating your own visions of London in 2126. Hands-on craft activities, design the skyline, meet time-travelling artist Montague Black and other drop in activities for children of all ages. Free with normal admission.
activities.whyItWorks: Hands-on activities and a focus on creativity will engage children.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria Line to Green Park, then Piccadilly Line to Covent Garden.
transport.tip: Check for special events and activities.

activities.title: Your Toys
activities.description: Children are invited to bring along a toy to lend for the performance and watch it come to life on stage, plus there's 10 minutes of playtime at the end.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive element of bringing toys makes it very engaging for children.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Shoreditch High Street then train to London Bridge.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Spring Family Fun at the Southbank Centre
activities.price: Some paid activities and some free
activities.description: A festival of fun and creativity for families including super shows, musical adventures, amazing puppetry and lots of free fun in the Easter break.
activities.whyItWorks: Wide variety of events and activities to suit different interests and ages.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria Line to Embankment, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Check the schedule for specific events and booking details.

activities.title: REPLAY: A Limitless Recycled Playground
activities.description: A play space made out of repurposed materials.
activities.whyItWorks: Creative and sustainable play space.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria Line to Embankment, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.
